purpose: The annotation-function return-shape reject arm (slice-2 review L3): bound_triple registers cleanly (2-arg, the arity gate sees nothing wrong) but returns a 3-tuple, and reason() dies at the annotate seam with ValueError('size mismatch for tuple, expected 2 element(s) but got 3') — the pinned objmode block coerces the registrand return to Tuple((float64, float64)) at its exit (interpretation.py:1918), so the wrong SIZE is rejected with the tuple-unbox message. The raise is the reason step's banked outcome; get_time after it compares the state the raise left behind.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_annotation_function (return-shape arm, named unobserved until session 29); interpretation.py:1914-1931 at the pin; reference fn harness/reference_fns.py bound_triple; screened live 2026-07-12 (screen_pin_arms.py ann-triple, 2 fresh pin processes, message byte-stable)
